name: Senda del Chorro Trail
name_original: Monasterio de San Pelayo de Cerrato — circular desde Cevico Navero
description: This moderate 13.5-mile mountain biking trail in Valles del Cerrato offers diverse terrain and views of the Conde del Guadalhorce reservoirs
region_intro: Mountain biking around Cevico Navero offers routes through the rolling hills and agricultural landscapes of the Cerrato region. The terrain features moderate elevation changes, suitable for various skill levels. Trails often traverse open fields, wooded sections, and areas with historical or geological interest, such as gypsum mines. The region provides a mix of natural beauty and cultural landmarks for mountain bikers to explore. Best mountain bike trails around Cevico Navero The most popular mountain bike trail is Baltanás a Hornillos de Cerrato — circular por las Minas de Yeso, a 20.5 miles (32.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 38 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the unique landscape around the gypsum mines. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Senda del Chorro Trail, a moderate 13.5 miles (21.7 km) path. This trail offers varied terrain and can be completed in about 1 hour 54 minutes. Local mountain bikers also love the Hérmedes de Cerrato a Castrillo de Don Juan — circular, a 17.9 miles (28.8 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 4 minutes. Mountain biking around Cevico Navero is defined by rolling hills, agricultural fields, and historical points of interest. The network offers options for easy mountain bike trails Cevico Navero and more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Cevico Navero are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 3 reviews. More than 17 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cevico Navero's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Cevico Navero?
A: There are currently 8 mountain bike trails documented on komoot around Cevico Navero, offering a variety of experiences through the region's rolling hills and agricultural landscapes.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Cevico Navero?
A: The trails around Cevico Navero are predominantly moderate, with 6 out of 8 routes falling into this category. There are also 2 easy routes, making the area accessible for various skill levels. There are no difficult trails listed.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Cevico Navero?
A: Yes, for those seeking an easier ride, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1323598678/source-santiago-from-baltanas-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source Santiago from Baltanas - circular&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent option. This easy route covers 13.3 km and typically takes around 1 hour 5 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available in Cevico Navero?
A: Many of the mountain bike routes in Cevico Navero are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1323726340/baltanas-a-hornillos-de-cerrato-circular-por-las-minas-de-yeso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baltanás a Hornillos de Cerrato — circular por las Minas de Yeso&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1324172694/hermedes-de-cerrato-a-castrillo-de-don-juan-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hérmedes de Cerrato a Castrillo de Don Juan — circular&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the mountain bike trails in Cevico Navero?
A: Mountain biking in Cevico Navero offers a blend of rolling hills, expansive agricultural fields, and wooded sections. You'll also encounter areas with historical and geological interest, such as the unique gypsum mines, providing a diverse and engaging landscape.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see near the mountain bike trails?
A: While riding, you can explore several points of interest. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5797861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baltanás Underground Wine Cellars&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5797677" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Miguel Arcángel and Rollo de Vertavillo&lt;/a&gt;, or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4384200" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Zúñiga Family&lt;/a&gt; in Encinas de Esgueva.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Cevico Navero?
A: The mountain bike trails in Cevico Navero are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, the mix of natural beauty and cultural landmarks, and the well-maintained paths that make for an enjoyable ride.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by historical or unique geological sites?
A: Yes, the region is known for its historical and geological interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1323726340/baltanas-a-hornillos-de-cerrato-circular-por-las-minas-de-yeso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baltanás a Hornillos de Cerrato — circular por las Minas de Yeso&lt;/a&gt; trail, for instance, specifically explores the unique landscape around the gypsum mines, offering a distinctive riding experience.

Q: What is the typical duration for a mountain bike ride in this area?
A: The duration of mountain bike rides in Cevico Navero varies depending on the trail. Easy routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1323598678/source-santiago-from-baltanas-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source Santiago from Baltanas - circular&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in just over an hour, while moderate trails such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1324800471/monastery-of-san-pelayo-cevico-navero-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Pelayo - Cevico Navero Loop&lt;/a&gt; typically take around 2 hours 17 minutes.

Q: Is Cevico Navero a good destination for mountain bikers looking for moderate challenges?
A: Absolutely. With 6 out of 8 routes classified as moderate, Cevico Navero is an excellent destination for mountain bikers seeking engaging rides with moderate elevation changes. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1323629735/senda-del-chorro-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Chorro Trail&lt;/a&gt; offer varied terrain perfect for intermediate riders.

Q: How many mountain bikers have explored Cevico Navero using komoot?
A: More than 17 mountain bikers have already used komoot to explore the varied terrain and trails around Cevico Navero, contributing to the community's high ratings for the area.
tips:
Romanesque-style Catholic temple dating from the 12th century, although it has been reformed several times over the centuries.
It is interesting to observe the main façade with the cover that still presents its Romanesque origins.

Architectural complex dating from the 10th century. Although for many years it was forgotten and its state was left in ruins, in 2007 a rehabilitation project was carried out to bring it back to life and give this location a social and cultural use for the inhabitants of Cevico Navero.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Senda del Chorro Trail?
faqs.answer: This trail offers a diverse mountain biking experience. You can expect a mix of terrain, including technical singletrack, rocky sections, and loose ground. While many trails in the region are natural and ungroomed, utilizing ancient rights of way, there are also fast and flowing sections through pine-forested hills. Be prepared for varied surfaces that can be dry, dusty, and rocky.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The Senda del Chorro Trail is approximately 21.7 kilometers long and typically takes around 1 hour and 55 minutes to complete by mountain bike. This duration can vary based on your fitness level, speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is the Senda del Chorro Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The trail is graded as 'moderate' in difficulty. While it requires a reasonable level of fitness, it does not demand a high level of technical mountain biking experience, making it accessible for riders beyond absolute beginners. However, some sections may be challenging due to rocky or loose terrain.

faqs.question: Where does the Senda del Chorro Trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting and ending near Cevico Navero, specifically from the area around the Monasterio de San Pelayo de Cerrato. Parking options would typically be found in or near Cevico Navero, which serves as a convenient access point for this circular route.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the Senda del Chorro Trail, you'll encounter historical and religious landmarks. Notable points of interest include the Church of Our Lady of Peace in Cevico Navero and the historic Monastery of San Pelayo. The route also offers scenic views through pine-forested hills and the natural landscapes of the Valles del Cerrato.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Senda del Chorro Trail?
faqs.answer: The region generally offers a good year-round riding climate. However, for the most comfortable experience, consider visiting during the spring (April-May) or autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder. Summers can be hot, and winters might bring cooler, potentially wetter conditions, though the trails are often dry and dusty for much of the year.

faqs.question: Is the Senda del Chorro Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact trail are not detailed, generally, dogs are permitted on trails in natural areas like Valles del Cerrato. It is always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety and comfort of other trail users. Please be mindful of any signage regarding dog access.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee to access the trail in Valles del Cerrato?
faqs.answer: As a natural trail within the Valles del Cerrato area, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required to mountain bike the Senda del Chorro Trail. However, it's always good practice to check local regulations before your visit, especially if you plan to access any specific protected areas or private lands.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints or particularly scenic spots along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail passes through pine-forested hills, offering various scenic vistas of the surrounding natural landscape in Valles del Cerrato. While specific named viewpoints are not listed, the circular nature of the route ensures diverse perspectives of the area, including glimpses of the historical landmarks mentioned.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or water sources near the Senda del Chorro Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends near Cevico Navero, a small village. You may find basic amenities like cafes or small shops there. It's always advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks for your ride, as direct access to amenities on the trail itself is unlikely. Plan to resupply in Cevico Navero before or after your ride.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is recommended for the Senda del Chorro Trail?
faqs.answer: Given the 'moderate' difficulty and varied terrain, including rocky and loose sections, a mountain bike with good suspension is recommended. A full-suspension bike would offer the most comfort and capability, especially on chunkier sections, but a capable hardtail could also manage for experienced riders.
